---
title: "Comparing groups"
description: "Stack prior and posterior draws with compare_draws()"
status: stable
---

`compare_draws()` calls `parameter_draws()` once per group (default `["posterior", "prior"]`), tags each with a `source` column, and stacks them into a single `DataFrame`. The result is the tidy input for any comparison plot — no manual concatenation.

## Setup

A `DataTree` with both a `posterior` and a `prior` group for `beta[groups]` and the scalar `sigma`.

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
import numpy as np
import polars as pl
import arviz as az
import tidydraws as td
from xarray import DataArray
from lets_plot import (
    LetsPlot,
    aes,
    facet_wrap,
    geom_density,
    geom_hline,
    geom_pointrange,
    ggplot,
    labs,
    position_dodge,
    theme_minimal,
)
LetsPlot.setup_html()

np.random.seed(11)
chains, draws, n_groups = 4, 500, 4
beta_post = np.random.normal(1.0, 0.2, (chains, draws, n_groups))
beta_prior = np.random.normal(0.0, 0.5, (chains, draws, n_groups))
sigma_post = np.random.normal(0.8, 0.1, (chains, draws))
sigma_prior = np.random.normal(1.0, 0.2, (chains, draws))

dt = az.from_dict(
    {
        "posterior": {
            "beta": DataArray(beta_post, dims=["chain", "draw", "groups"]),
            "sigma": DataArray(sigma_post, dims=["chain", "draw"]),
        },
        "prior": {
            "beta": DataArray(beta_prior, dims=["chain", "draw", "groups"]),
            "sigma": DataArray(sigma_prior, dims=["chain", "draw"]),
        },
    },
    coords={"groups": [f"g{i}" for i in range(n_groups)]},
    dims={"beta": ["groups"]},
)
```

One call stacks both sources:

```{python}
compare = td.compare_draws(dt, "beta[groups]")
compare.head()
```

## Gallery

### Prior vs posterior (pooled)

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Prior vs posterior density of `beta`, pooled across groups."
(
    ggplot(compare.to_pandas(), aes("beta", fill="source"))
    + geom_density(alpha=0.5)
    + labs(x="beta", y="density", fill="source",
           title="Prior vs posterior (pooled)")
    + theme_minimal()
)
```

### Prior vs posterior by group

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Prior vs posterior density of `beta`, faceted by group."
(
    ggplot(compare.to_pandas(), aes("beta", fill="source"))
    + geom_density(alpha=0.5)
    + facet_wrap(facets="groups", ncol=2)
    + labs(x="beta", y="density", fill="source",
           title="Prior vs posterior by group")
    + theme_minimal()
)
```

### Credible-interval comparison

A different cut: 89% intervals per group, dodged by source — the shrinkage from prior to posterior reads off at a glance.

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Prior vs posterior 89% credible intervals of `beta` by group."
ci = (
    compare.group_by("groups", "source")
    .agg(
        pl.col("beta").quantile(0.055).alias("lower"),
        pl.col("beta").median().alias("median"),
        pl.col("beta").quantile(0.945).alias("upper"),
    )
    .sort("groups", "source")
)

(
    ggplot(ci.to_pandas(), aes("groups", "median", color="source"))
    + geom_pointrange(
        aes(ymin="lower", ymax="upper"),
        position=position_dodge(0.5), size=0.8,
    )
    + geom_hline(yintercept=0, linetype="dashed", color="#888888")
    + labs(x="group", y="beta (89% CrI)", color="source",
           title="Prior vs posterior credible intervals")
    + theme_minimal()
)
```

`group_name` sets the source column's name, and `groups` accepts any pair of
`DataTree` groups — not just prior/posterior. See
[`parameter_draws()`](parameter_draws.qmd) for the underlying extraction.
